pi_items.source: Export logic in task.json explicitly requests this path.
Python calculation of the synthetic flight schedule injected in setup_task.sh confirms exactly 1 rest violation for Crew 101 between Flight 1001 and 1002 (7.5 hours).

Python simulation of the 28-day rolling flight block hours calculation on the synthetic schedule from setup_task.sh confirms exactly 1 limit violation for Crew 102 (Flight 2012 rolling hours = 108 > 100).
Task instructions and export_result.sh explicitly test for LEAD, RANGE BETWEEN, creating a PL/SQL procedure, and calculating date differences.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically generated flight schedules directly injected via setup_task.sh SQL statements. It has been verified that the expected output path explicitly matches the task instructions. Furthermore, mathematical verification of the injected schedules confirms there is exactly 1 expected rest violation (Crew 101 with 7.5 hours rest) and exactly 1 expected 28-day limit violation (Crew 102 with 108 rolling hours), which precisely matches the explicit requirements and verifier logic.
